subject: Biology
title: Problem Solving and Insight
description: A 60-second lesson on Problem Solving and Insight.

hook.text: A crow drops stones into a tube of water to raise the level and reach a floating treat.

buildup.text: Problem solving requires more than trial and error—it involves understanding cause and effect.

discovery.text: Kea parrots in New Zealand solve multi-step puzzles that stump many primates.

twist.text: Octopuses unscrew jar lids from the inside. They can solve mazes and remember solutions for months.

climax.text: Insight—suddenly grasping a solution without trial and error—has been demonstrated in apes and corvids.

punchline.text: Think first, act once. That's insight.

quiz.question: Which animal can unscrew jar lids from the inside?
